title: Shoulder pain
source_review_basis: committed exact-section summaries and evidence records; full guideline documents were not retained for this pass

red_flags.text: Requires direct clinician-patient decisions and lists infection, unreduced dislocation, tumour, traumatic acute cuff tear, and inflammatory disease as urgent concern contexts.
red_flags.rationale: Directly supports mapped infection, trauma, dislocation, cuff-tear, malignancy, inflammatory, and severe-function concern prompts.
red_flags.evidence_extract: Requires direct clinician-patient decisions and lists infection, unreduced dislocation, tumour, traumatic acute cuff tear, and inflammatory disease as urgent concern contexts.

source.title: Shoulder Pain: Diagnosis, Treatment and Referral Guidelines for Primary, Community and Intermediate Care

exact_section.heading: Shared Decision Making and Urgent Red Flag Referrals for the Shoulder

red_flags.text: Supports rotator-cuff or impingement terminology and history covering hand dominance, occupation, activity, pain location, radiation, onset, duration, aggravating factors, trauma, comorbidities, red flags, and passive external rotation.
red_flags.rationale: Directly supports mapped shoulder and rotator-cuff terminology and pain, movement, weakness, trauma, function, and range-of-motion history.
red_flags.evidence_extract: Supports rotator-cuff or impingement terminology and history covering hand dominance, occupation, activity, pain location, radiation, onset, duration, aggravating factors, trauma, comorbidities, red flags, and passive external rotation.

source.title: Shoulder Pain: Diagnosis, Treatment and Referral Guidelines for Primary, Community and Intermediate Care

exact_section.heading: Subacromial Shoulder Pain — definition and diagnosis

investigations.text: Supports selected two-view radiography, states ultrasound or MRI is rarely indicated without an agreed specialist pathway, and links persistent symptoms to clinician-arranged referral.
investigations.rationale: Directly supports mapped selected imaging and clinician-arranged referral or follow-up documentation.
investigations.evidence_extract: Supports selected two-view radiography, states ultrasound or MRI is rarely indicated without an agreed specialist pathway, and links persistent symptoms to clinician-arranged referral.

source.title: Shoulder Pain: Diagnosis, Treatment and Referral Guidelines for Primary, Community and Intermediate Care

exact_section.heading: Subacromial Shoulder Pain — imaging and referral

investigations.text: Supports range-of-motion, stiffness, function, instability, trauma, recurrent dislocation, and clinician-selected imaging or referral documentation across common shoulder presentations.
investigations.rationale: Directly supports mapped stiffness, instability, trauma, range, external rotation, and recurrent-dislocation contexts across common shoulder presentations.
investigations.evidence_extract: Supports range-of-motion, stiffness, function, instability, trauma, recurrent dislocation, and clinician-selected imaging or referral documentation across common shoulder presentations.

source_limitations: Neer, Hawkins-Kennedy, drop-arm, apprehension, Jobe, lift-off, and other individual test accuracy are not established by the reviewed sections.
UAE imaging/referral pathways, antibiotics, injections, analgesia, heat/ice, and fixed follow-up remain unsupported.
The rotator-cuff-injury diagnosis label remains clinician-stated.
Qualified clinician review has not been completed.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
